raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 660 standardizes confusing date labels that result in unnecessary food waste across California.",
    "There are more than 50 differently phrased date labels on packaged food in grocery stores.",
    "Inconsistent date labels mislead consumers into thinking products are no longer safe to eat.",
    "AB 660 requires manufacturers to use 'BEST if Used By' for peak quality and 'Use By' for product safety.",
    "The use of consumer-facing 'sell by' dates will be prohibited under AB 660.",
    "Californians throw away six million tons of food waste each year, with date label confusion being a leading cause.",
    "Decomposing food in landfills accounts for 41 percent of California's point-source methane emissions.",
    "Methane is 84 times more powerful than carbon dioxide as a driver of global warming.",
    "Minimizing food waste is integral to meeting California's greenhouse gas reduction goals.",
    "The average American spends $1,300 on food that is never eaten.",
    "The misleading date labels cost Californians billions each year.",
    "Date label standardization is a commonsense solution that can reduce climate warming emissions.",
    "Recent years have seen an increase in momentum surrounding date label standardization efforts.",
    "AB 660 represents a significant legislative precedent for food waste reduction efforts at the national level.",
    "Standardizing food date labels is considered an effective method to reduce food waste and protect public health."
  ]
}
```
